---
title: "Introducing the Outbreak"
id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01-sec01-sp01"
section_id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01-sec01"
session_id: "OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01"
toc: True
---











<!-- IMPORT_START -->

An outbreak of **measles**, a highly contagious viral disease, occurred in a semi-urban community with mixed vaccination coverage. Measles is one of the most transmissible infectious diseases, with a basic reproduction number (R₀) often estimated between 12 and 18.

Public health teams initiated an outbreak investigation to:

- identify confirmed and suspected cases

- determine vaccination status

- assess transmission patterns

- estimate the **real-world effectiveness of the vaccine**

For an overview of measles epidemiology, see:\
<https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/measles>

## Understanding Vaccine Effectiveness

Vaccine effectiveness (VE) refers to how well a vaccine performs in real-world conditions, outside controlled clinical trials.

Unlike vaccine efficacy, effectiveness reflects:

- population diversity

- variation in exposure

- real-world healthcare access

- behavioural factors

👉 Learn more:\
<https://www.who.int/news-room/feature-stories/detail/vaccine-efficacy-effectiveness-and-protection>

## Why This Matters in Public Health

Estimating vaccine effectiveness is essential for:

- evaluating vaccination programmes

- identifying vulnerable populations

- informing outbreak response

- guiding policy decisions

For further reading:\
<https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6734418/>

##  Key Concept

::: {.callout-important}
Vaccine effectiveness measures the reduction in disease risk among vaccinated individuals compared to unvaccinated individuals in real-world conditions.
:::

## Observed Data

The following table summarises the number of cases and non-cases among vaccinated and unvaccinated individuals during the outbreak.

  --------------------------------------------------------
  **Group**      **Cases (a, c)**   **Non-cases (b, d)**
  -------------- ------------------ ----------------------
  Vaccinated     4                  96

  Unvaccinated   20                 80
  --------------------------------------------------------

Table: Summary of outbreak cases by vaccination status

## Formula for Vaccine Effectiveness

Vaccine effectiveness is calculated as:

VE = (1 − Relative Risk) × 100

## Step-by-Step Calculation

<details>
<summary><strong>Show steps</strong></summary>

- Step 1 :: Calculate risk in the vaccinated group: 5 / 500 = 0.01
- Step 2 :: Calculate risk in the unvaccinated group: 25 / 500 = 0.05
- Step 3 :: Relative Risk = 0.01 / 0.05 = 0.2
- Step 4 :: VE = (1 − 0.2) × 100 = 80%

</details>

## Pause and Reflect

::: {.callout-tip}
**Self-check:** Why might vaccine effectiveness differ between populations?

**Suggested answer**

Differences in exposure, population structure, healthcare access, and underlying health conditions can influence estimates.
:::

## Youtube

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/yt3e8Ng0mf0" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>


## Panopto

<iframe src="https://lshtm.cloud.panopto.eu/Panopto/Pages/Embed.aspx?id=d19ba573-9ad1-480b-95db-b3ed01014aab" title="Panopto video player" width="720" height="405" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>


## Visualising the Results

```{r}
#| fig-alt: "Bar chart showing risk of infection for vaccinated and unvaccinated groups."
#| fig-cap: "Risk of Infection by Vaccination Status"
group <- c("Vaccinated", "Unvaccinated")
cases <- c(5, 25)
population <- c(500, 500)
risk <- cases / population
barplot(risk, names.arg = group, col = c("steelblue", "tomato"),
main = "Risk of Infection by Vaccination Status")
```

## R Code Only

```r
group <- c("Vaccinated", "Unvaccinated")
cases <- c(5, 25)
population <- c(500, 500)
risk <- cases / population
barplot(risk, names.arg = group, col = c("steelblue", "tomato"),
main = "Risk of Infection by Vaccination Status")
```

## Interpreting the Results

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Interpretation
An 80% vaccine effectiveness means vaccinated individuals have substantially lower risk compared to unvaccinated individuals.

## Assumptions
The calculation assumes both groups are comparable and equally exposed.

## Limitations
Confounding factors such as age, immunity, or healthcare access may influence results.

:::
## Epidemic Curve

![Epidemic curve showing number of measles cases over time by vaccination status](../../resources/images/epidemic-curve.png){width='70%'}
Figure 1. Epidemic curve comparing vaccinated and unvaccinated groups.

## Outbreak Report

<iframe src="../../resources/pdf/outbreak-report.pdf" title="Embedded PDF: View full outbreak investigation report" width="100%" height="600" loading="lazy"></iframe>

[View full outbreak investigation report](../../resources/pdf/outbreak-report.pdf)

## Download Dataset

[Download full dataset](../../resources/data/outbreak-dataset.zip)

## Quiz

Based on the outbreak data, what does a vaccine effectiveness of 80% mean in practice?

<form class="quiz-block" data-quiz="quiz_1">
<div class="quiz-option"><input type="radio" id="quiz_1_opt_1" name="quiz_1" value="Vaccinated individuals have zero risk of infection"> <label for="quiz_1_opt_1">Vaccinated individuals have zero risk of infection</label></div>
<div class="quiz-option"><input type="radio" id="quiz_1_opt_2" name="quiz_1" value="Vaccinated individuals have an 80% lower risk of infection than unvaccinated individuals"> <label for="quiz_1_opt_2">Vaccinated individuals have an 80% lower risk of infection than unvaccinated individuals</label></div>
<div class="quiz-option"><input type="radio" id="quiz_1_opt_3" name="quiz_1" value="80% of vaccinated individuals will not become infected"> <label for="quiz_1_opt_3">80% of vaccinated individuals will not become infected</label></div>
<div class="quiz-option"><input type="radio" id="quiz_1_opt_4" name="quiz_1" value="The vaccine prevents 80 cases in every outbreak regardless of context"> <label for="quiz_1_opt_4">The vaccine prevents 80 cases in every outbreak regardless of context</label></div>
</form>

<details>
<summary><strong>Show answer</strong></summary>

**Answer:** Vaccinated individuals have an 80% lower risk of infection than unvaccinated individuals

**Explanation:**

Vaccine effectiveness compares the risk of disease in vaccinated and unvaccinated groups under real-world conditions. An 80% VE means the vaccinated group experienced substantially lower risk, not that infection risk was eliminated entirely.
##

</details>

## Key Takeaway

::: {.callout-tip}
Vaccination significantly reduces the likelihood of infection and severe disease, even if it does not eliminate risk entirely.
:::

## Further Reading

WHO Measles Fact Sheet : <https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/measles>\
Vaccine Effectiveness Overview : <https://www.who.int/news-room/feature-stories/detail/vaccine-efficacy-effectiveness-and-protection>\
Measles Vaccine Impact Study : <https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6734418/>

<!-- IMPORT_END -->
<!-- Standard workflow: content imported from Word -->
<!-- Advanced users may edit directly -->

<!-- START_NAVIGATION -->


```{=html}
<div class="custom-page-navigation">
    <div class="nav-prev">
        
        <a href="index.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">← Previous</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Outbreak Context</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
    <div class="nav-next">
        
        <a href="OUTBREAK_VE_DEMO-se01-sec01-sp02.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">Next →</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Why Vaccine Effectiveness Matters</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
</div>
```

<!-- END_NAVIGATION -->
